outcome: T2DM remission (HbA1c <48 mmol/mol off all glucose-lowering drugs >=3 mo)
indirectness: serious (both trials China only; no Africa/South-Asia/LATAM/MENA data)
imprecision: very serious (k=2; very wide CIs; few events)
other_considerations: both trials upper-middle-income China; no dose-response/large-effect upgrade applied given imprecision
plain_language_summary: Structured dietary lifestyle modification probably increases drug-free T2DM remission versus control, but the LMIC evidence is limited to two small Chinese RCTs; the true effect is very uncertain and not generalisable to African or other LMIC settings.

outcome: Drug-free BP normalisation (<130/80 off antihypertensives >=6 mo) [exploratory]
other_considerations: No eligible LMIC RCT reported the drug-free BP-normalisation endpoint; the India Digital-Twin trial reported HTN remission (50% vs 0%) as a secondary analysis but is a single, digitally-delivered trial
plain_language_summary: No LMIC RCT meeting the protocol definition reported sustained drug-free blood-pressure normalisation; this is an explicit evidence gap.

other_considerations: Qingdao LCD vs LFD RCT reported reduced medication-effect scores but not drug-free remission
plain_language_summary: One Chinese RCT reported greater reduction in glucose-lowering medication with a low-carbohydrate diet, but did not report complete drug-free remission.
